top_headlines.headline: U.S.-Iran Two-Week Ceasefire Takes Hold But Remains Fragile as Lebanon Strikes Continue
top_headlines.summary: The United States and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire brokered by Pakistan, halting more than 40 days of U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran in exchange for Tehran reopening the Strait of Hormuz to shipping. The agreement is described as fragile: Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps claimed Hormuz shipping had stopped again after continued Israeli strikes on Lebanon, which both Washington and Jerusalem say are not covered by the ceasefire. Delegations from both sides are expected to meet in Islamabad on Friday to negotiate a more lasting accord.

top_headlines.headline: Global Markets Surge and Oil Plunges on Iran Ceasefire News; Energy Prices Remain Elevated
top_headlines.summary: Oil prices fell as much as 16% following the ceasefire announcement, with U.S. crude dropping to around $94 per barrel — still more than 40% above pre-war levels. Wall Street posted its largest single-day Dow gain since April 2025, while Asian and European benchmarks also surged sharply. Analysts cautioned that the relief rally may be short-lived given the ceasefire's fragility, unresolved trust deficits, and a backlog of 187 tankers stranded in the Gulf.

top_headlines.headline: Democrat-Backed Chris Taylor Wins Wisconsin Supreme Court Race, Expanding Liberal Majority to 5-2
top_headlines.summary: Wisconsin appeals court judge Chris Taylor defeated Republican-backed Maria Lazar by approximately 20 percentage points in the April 7 state Supreme Court election, the fourth consecutive victory for liberal-backed candidates in the battleground state. Taylor's win expands the court's liberal majority from 4-3 to 5-2, cementing liberal control until at least 2030. The race attracted significantly less national spending than Wisconsin's previous two high-profile judicial contests.

tech_ai.headline: Meta Debuts Muse Spark, Its First Proprietary AI Model From Superintelligence Labs
tech_ai.summary: Meta unveiled Muse Spark, the first AI model produced by its Meta Superintelligence Labs unit led by Chief AI Officer Alexandr Wang, marking a pivot away from the company's prior open-source Llama strategy. The model, described as competitive with leading models from OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google on several benchmarks, will power Meta AI across Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, and Meta's Ray-Ban glasses in the coming weeks. Meta's planned AI capital expenditures for 2026 are projected at between $115 billion and $135 billion, nearly double its 2025 spending.

tech_ai.headline: Anthropic Restricts New Claude Mythos Model to Cybersecurity Defense, Citing Power and Risk
tech_ai.summary: Anthropic is limiting access to its new Claude Mythos Preview model, steering it toward defensive cybersecurity work rather than a broad public release, after the model demonstrated the ability to identify thousands of zero-day vulnerabilities across major operating systems and browsers. The company launched Project Glasswing with partners including Amazon, Apple, Google, Microsoft, and Nvidia to use the model to identify and fix high-severity software vulnerabilities. The move signals a growing focus among frontier AI labs on controlling deployment of their most capable models.

tech_ai.headline: Greece to Ban Social Media for Under-15s from 2027, Calls for EU-Wide Framework
tech_ai.summary: Greece announced it will prohibit children under the age of 15 from accessing social media platforms starting January 1, 2027, citing concerns about anxiety, sleep disruption, and addictive design. Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis also called on the European Commission to establish an EU-wide framework for age verification and enforcement. The move is one of Europe's most sweeping national-level digital restrictions targeting minors.

business_finance.headline: Oil Below $100 After Iran Ceasefire, But Prices Remain Far Above Pre-War Levels
business_finance.summary: Brent crude fell 13% to settle around $94.80 per barrel and U.S. WTI crude dropped more than 15% following the U.S.-Iran ceasefire announcement, though both benchmarks remain more than 40% above the roughly $70 per barrel price seen before the conflict began in late February. The Ras Laffan LNG complex in Qatar suffered damage reducing its export capacity by 17%, and analysts estimate the cost of rebuilding Middle East energy infrastructure could exceed $25 billion. The International Air Transport Association warned it could take months for jet fuel supplies and prices to normalize even if the Strait of Hormuz stays open.

business_finance.headline: Wall Street Posts Biggest Rally in a Year on Iran Ceasefire; S&P 500 Closes Up 2.5%
business_finance.summary: The S&P 500 rose 2.5%, the Nasdaq gained 2.8%, and the Dow Jones jumped 1,325 points on Wednesday — the index's largest single-day percentage gain since April 2025. Asian indices led by South Korea's Kospi surged nearly 7%, while Germany's DAX jumped more than 5% and Europe's Stoxx 600 rose 3.8%. JPMorgan strategists wrote that the S&P 500 could climb further "as euphoria returns to markets," though investors remain cautious given unresolved questions about the ceasefire's durability.

business_finance.headline: FedEx Reaches Tentative Deal With Pilots' Union After Five Years of Negotiations
business_finance.summary: FedEx and its pilots' union reached a tentative agreement, ending more than five years of contract negotiations, according to Bloomberg. The deal comes as the air freight and logistics industry faces elevated operating costs linked to surging jet fuel prices stemming from the disruption to Strait of Hormuz tanker traffic. Additional details of the agreement had not been disclosed at time of publication.

world_news.headline: Vietnam's National Assembly Elects Le Minh Hung as New Prime Minister for 2026-2031 Term
world_news.summary: Vietnam's 16th National Assembly unanimously elected Politburo member Le Minh Hung as the country's new prime minister on April 7, with all 495 legislators present voting in favor. Hung, 56, a former Governor of the State Bank of Vietnam, replaces outgoing Premier Pham Minh Chinh and pledged to target annual economic growth of at least 10% driven by innovation and digital transformation. The same session confirmed party General Secretary To Lam as State President, consolidating significant political authority in a single figure.

world_news.headline: UK's Wireless Festival Cancelled After Home Office Bans Kanye West From Entering Country
world_news.summary: London's Wireless Festival, scheduled for July 10-12 at Finsbury Park, was cancelled after the UK Home Office withdrew Ye's (formerly Kanye West) Electronic Travel Authorisation on the grounds that his presence would not be "conducive to the public good," citing his history of antisemitic statements. Festival organisers confirmed all ticket holders will receive automatic full refunds. Prime Minister Keir Starmer said the artist "should never have been invited to headline Wireless."

world_news.headline: VP Vance to Lead U.S. Delegation to Islamabad for Iran Peace Talks Beginning Saturday
world_news.summary: Vice President JD Vance, along with special envoy Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, are set to travel to Islamabad, Pakistan, for negotiations aimed at converting the two-week U.S.-Iran ceasefire into a more permanent agreement. Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif, whose government brokered the ceasefire, invited both delegations to Islamabad and called for a broader cessation of hostilities. Significant gaps remain between the two sides, particularly over uranium enrichment, sanctions, and the scope of U.S. military presence in the region.

music.headline: Rolling Stones Tease New Album 'Foreign Tongues' With Cryptic 'The Cockroaches' Campaign Ahead of April 11 Reveal
music.summary: Mysterious posters bearing the name 'The Cockroaches' — a pseudonym the Rolling Stones have used for secret shows since 1977 — appeared across London this week, directing fans to thecockroaches.com, a site registered to Universal Music, the band's label. Reports indicate the Stones plan to officially announce their 25th studio album, reportedly titled 'Foreign Tongues,' with lead single 'Mr Charm' on Saturday, April 11. Guitarist Ronnie Wood confirmed new music was coming in 2026, though a full tour has been ruled out due to Keith Richards's scheduling constraints.

music.headline: Bleachers Announce New Album 'Everyone For 10 Minutes' and Kick Off Rolling Stone Rock Tour
music.summary: Bleachers have announced their fifth studio album, 'Everyone For 10 Minutes,' due May 22 via Dirty Hit, alongside lead singles 'You And Forever' and 'Dirty Wedding Dress.' The band will headline the first stop of the 2026 Rolling Stone Rock Tour at the Fillmore New Orleans on May 20 — two nights before the album's release — with public tickets on sale today, April 9. Bleachers also announced a major UK and European headline tour for November–December 2026, their largest overseas run to date.

music.headline: Wireless Festival Collapse Renews Debate Over Artists With Hate Speech Records Booking Major UK Festivals
music.summary: The sudden cancellation of Wireless Festival following the UK government's ban on Ye has prompted broader discussion in the British music industry about due-diligence processes when booking controversial headliners. Sponsors Pepsi and Diageo had already withdrawn before the cancellation, and the Jewish Leadership Council, London Mayor Sadiq Khan, and Prime Minister Keir Starmer all publicly condemned the booking. Wireless organisers acknowledged that 'multiple stakeholders were consulted in advance' and that 'no concerns were highlighted at the time.'
